title: Services, Units, Boot State, and Logs
description: Diagnose a service that is enabled but unavailable by separating desired boot state, current process state, dependencies, configuration, and logs. Learners cumulatively produce a service lifecycle and journal evidence packet through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Relate service managers, units, dependencies, targets, process supervision, and application readiness.
Use status, is-active, is-enabled, show, list-dependencies, and failed-unit evidence before changing service state.
Query journal fields, units, boots, priorities, time ranges, cursors, and follow mode without losing causal context.
